_note: Grounding data for sampleCareers.json — official BLS OOH + O*NET references. Pay figures are May 2024 medians from BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook Quick Facts; outlook phrases use BLS 2024–34 projection language. INCREMENTAL DRAFT: claimChecks pending verification.

careers.outlook: Much faster than average (29% growth projected 2024–34)

claimChecks.note: BLS projects 29% growth 2024–34, much faster than average.

careers.medianPay: $60,340 (May 2024 median, BLS — computer user support specialists)
careers.outlook: Projected to decline 3% 2024–34; still ~50,500 openings/yr from replacement needs

claimChecks.note: BLS projects a 3% employment DECLINE 2024–34; openings (~50,500/yr) come from replacement needs, not growth. Suggest softening to 'Openings from turnover, not growth'.

careers.onetTitle: Management Analysts (closest proxy — federal civil service is an employer sector, not a single SOC occupation)
careers.medianPay: $101,190 (May 2024 median, BLS — management analysts, used as proxy; GS pay varies by grade/locality)
careers.outlook: Much faster than average (9% growth projected 2024–34 — proxy occupation)
careers.socMatchNote: No clean SOC match: 'federal civil service' spans many occupations. Management Analysts (program-analyst roles, per the career's keywords) used as the closest proxy.

careers.medianPay: $93,600 (May 2024 median, BLS — registered nurses; EMT entry rungs pay less)
careers.outlook: Faster than average (5% growth projected 2024–34); ~189,100 openings/yr
careers.socMatchNote: Career spans EMT (29-2042.00) → RN (29-1141.00); RN is the destination occupation and primary match.

careers.onetTitle: Secondary School Teachers, Except Special and Career/Technical Education
careers.outlook: Projected to decline 2% 2024–34; still ~66,200 openings/yr from replacement needs

claimChecks.note: BLS projects a 2% employment DECLINE for high school teachers 2024–34; openings (~66,200/yr) are replacement-driven. Corporate training (13-1151.00) is an alternate lane.
